title: Calibrating feature weights
text: ELSA prioritizations maximise weighted, additive utility: total amount feature secured, summed across planning units action zones. objective additive, spatially extensive features — features co-occur areas valuable features — favoured rarer isolated features every feature weighted equally. features end well represented others left far achieve. elsar_calibrate_weights() computes per-feature weight w_j counteracts , features achieve even representation relative single-feature maximum. reproduces ELSA calibration framework exactly; vignette shows run read result.

title: Calibrating feature weights
text: Single-feature maxima. feature j maximised (weight 1 j, 0 rest) find maximum attainable representation a_j^max. Baseline. problem solved equal weights get feature’s representation a_j relative shortfall delta_j = (a_j - a_j^max) / a_j^max. Iterate. Weights nudged — worst-represented feature, none best — problem re-solved, shortfalls recomputed. repeats allocation stops changing; weights giving even representation (smallest spread delta_j) returned. objective update rule unchanged original ELSA tool; implementation additionally returns best-seen weights, stops allocation freezes, excludes zero-total features.

title: Calibrating feature weights
text: build small synthetic stack four features different spatial footprints, two action zones, per-zone impacts budgets. Run calibration. use free HiGHS solver ; production typically use solver = "gurobi". calibrated weights: representation profile shows close feature gets single-feature maximum (ratio = / a_max); calibration lifts weakest features: per-iteration trajectory records spread shortfalls shrinks allocation froze:
code: library(elsar) library(terra) #> terra 1.9.34  set.seed(1) r0 <- rast(nrows = 24, ncols = 24, xmin = 0, xmax = 24, ymin = 0, ymax = 24,            crs = "EPSG:3857") xx <- init(r0, "x") yy <- init(r0, "y")  features <- c(   init(r0, runif),                          # broad, diffuse   (xx < 8) * 1,                             # concentrated strip   ((xx - 12)^2 + (yy - 12)^2 < 40) * 1,     # central disc   (yy > 20) * init(r0, runif)               # sparse band ) names(features) <- c("diffuse", "strip", "disc", "band")  pu_all   <- ifel(!is.na(features[[1]]), 1, NA) zones_pu <- list(protect = pu_all, restore = pu_all)  impacts <- data.frame(   feature = names(features),   protect = c(1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0),   restore = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.8, 0.5) ) budgets <- c(protect = 20, restore = 15)  # % of planning-unit area cal <- elsar_calibrate_weights(   features       = features,   impacts        = impacts,   zones_pu       = zones_pu,   planning_units = pu_all,   budgets        = budgets,   solver         = "highs",   verbose        = FALSE ) #> [2026-07-10 08:51:24] Calculated target: 115 (20% of total PU area) #> [2026-07-10 08:51:24] Calculated target: 86 (15% of total PU area) cal$weights #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   feature weight #>   <chr>    <dbl> #> 1 diffuse   2.10 #> 2 strip     7.46 #> 3 disc      7.17 #> 4 band     11.0 cal$representation #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   feature     a a_max ratio delta_pct #>   <chr>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 diffuse 0.367 0.490 0.748     -25.2 #> 2 strip   0.5   0.719 0.695     -30.5 #> 3 disc    0.690 0.992 0.696     -30.4 #> 4 band    0.674 1     0.674     -32.6 cal$trajectory[, c("iter", "spread_new", "max_abs_util_change", "weight_max")] #> # A tibble: 8 × 4 #>    iter spread_new max_abs_util_change weight_max #>   <int>      <dbl>               <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1     0      33.4             NA             1    #> 2     1      35.2              0.372         3    #> 3     2      30.7              0.302         3.02 #> 4     3       9.43             0.163         5.02 #> 5     4       9.67             0.0167        7.02 #> 6     5       8.92             0.00334       9.02 #> 7     6       7.41             0.00565      11.0  #> 8     7       7.41             0            13.0 cal$converged #> [1] TRUE cal$iterations #> [1] 7

title: Calibrating feature weights
text: run time ELSA tool multiplies calibration weights user-specified weights, w_j = w_j^user * w_j^cal (w_j^user = 1 default). Persist cal$weights alongside prepared layers load weight_calibration feature.

title: Calibrating feature weights
text: elsar_calibrate_weights() needs prioritizr supported solver: solver = "gurobi" — fastest; requires commercial gurobi R package. solver = "highs" — free open-source (install.packages("highs")). solver = "default" — prioritizr’s best available solver. Transient solver cloud-connection failures retried automatically (solve_retries).

title: Referencia de Procesamiento de Datos Globales
text: El paquete elsar proporciona funciones para procesar diversos conjuntos de datos globales para su uso en la planificacion de conservacion ELSA (Areas Esenciales de Soporte Vital). Esta tabla de referencia resume los conjuntos de datos disponibles, sus funciones R correspondientes y notas de procesamiento.

title: Referencia de Procesamiento de Datos Globales
text: Probado: Indica si la funcion ha sido probada con datos reales (Y = Si, N = ). Desde: La version de elsar en la que la funcion estuvo disponible por primera vez. La mayoria de los conjuntos de datos usan make_normalised_raster() para procesamiento estandar con parametros opcionales (p. ej., inverse = TRUE). Algunos conjuntos de datos requieren funciones especializadas (p. ej., make_kbas(), make_mangroves(), make_forest_integrity()). Los conjuntos de datos con “NA” como nombre tienen un archivo fuente global fijo y pueden variar segun el pais o el analisis.

title: Un Ejemplo de Flujo de Trabajo ELSA
code: # cargar paquetes library(elsar) library(prioritizr) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(sf) library(terra)  # establecer semilla para reproducibilidad set.seed(500)

title: Un Ejemplo de Flujo de Trabajo ELSA
text: Al inicio del analisis, es necesario definir la region de planificacion y crear unidades de planificacion para dicha region. En este ejemplo, utilizamos Nepal como caso de estudio para demostrar las funciones de este paquete. Para ello, primero obtenemos el limite del pais de Nepal utilizando su codigo iso3 (“NPL”). Normalmente, seria necesario proporcionar su propio conjunto de datos con los limites del pais para filtrar. Los datos pueden cargarse desde maquinas locales en formato vectorial o raster, o mediante una conexion remota traves de postgres utilizando la funcion elsar_load_data(). Aqui utilizamos un conjunto de datos previamente guardado. La funcion make_boundary() tambien ofrece la opcion de crear una proyeccion personalizada centrada en la region de planificacion. Esto tambien puede hacerse en pasos separados utilizando la funcion make_custom_projection(), que esta integrada en la funcion make_boundary(). Podemos verificar si los dos CRS de los limites coinciden.
code: # crear limite para Nepal boundary <- make_boundary(   boundary_in = boundary_dat,   iso3 = "NPL",   iso3_column = "iso3cd",   custom_projection = FALSE )  ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = boundary) boundary_proj <- make_boundary(   boundary_in = boundary_dat,   iso3 = "NPL",   iso3_column = "iso3cd",   custom_projection = TRUE )  # crear proyeccion personalizada y aplicarla al limite wkt <- make_custom_projection(boundary = boundary, iso3 = "NPL") boundary_proj2 <- sf::st_transform(boundary, crs = sf::st_crs(wkt)) # verificar crs print(sf::st_crs(boundary_proj) == sf::st_crs(boundary_proj2))

title: Un Ejemplo de Flujo de Trabajo ELSA
text: Para definir un problema de planificacion de conservacion, necesitamos subdividir la region de planificacion en unidades mas pequenas que pueden ser seleccionadas o en la priorizacion posterior y las cuales se puede atribuir la informacion de caracteristicas. La funcion tiene la opcion de agregar un tamano deseado de unidad de planificacion. Si se proporciona ninguno, la funcion busca hacer las unidades de planificacion lo mas pequenas posible manteniendo la eficiencia computacional. El valor predeterminado es de 850.000 unidades de planificacion, basado en experiencia previa con tiempos de ejecucion.
code: # crear unidades de planificacion pus <- make_planning_units(   boundary_proj = boundary_proj,   pu_size = NULL,   pu_threshold = 8.5e5,   limit_to_mainland = FALSE,   iso3 = "NPL" )

title: Un Ejemplo de Flujo de Trabajo ELSA
text: Las caracteristicas de biodiversidad son una parte integral de la planificacion de conservacion. Las caracteristicas pueden abarcar desde distribuciones de especies hasta conjuntos de datos mas generales como las Areas Clave para la Biodiversidad (KBA). En nuestro ejemplo, utilizaremos solo un conjunto de datos, el Convergence Global Change Issues del Atlas Mundial de la Desertificacion (WAD; ver https://wad.jrc.ec.europa.eu/). De nuevo, los datos de entrada pueden cargarse primero utilizando la funcion elsar_load_data() (aqui ya tenemos el conjunto de datos guardado en el paquete). Los datos luego necesitan ser reproyectados y remuestreados para coincidir exactamente con la resolucion y extension de las unidades de planificacion previamente creadas. Tambien normalizamos los datos para que solo tengan valores dentro del rango 0-1. En lugar de solo echar un vistazo rapido los datos usando la funcion plot del paquete terra, tambien podemos crear un grafico utilizando las opciones de visualizacion integradas en el paquete elsar, como elsar_plot_static_raster(), que permite graficar datos raster continuos con ggplot. Tambien podemos agregar datos de fondo al grafico y anadir propiedades adicionales para cambiar la apariencia del grafico utilizando la funcion elsar_plot_optics() que permite especificar un tema ggplot especifico. Tambien podemos cambiar la paleta de colores para que sea diferente de la predeterminada “viridis”. Si es necesario, se pueden incluir logotipos en el mapa. La ubicacion depende de las dimensiones del grafico y por lo tanto necesita ajustarse segun la figura, por ejemplo utilizando terra::ext(). Si estamos interesados en la distribucion, tambien podemos graficar la distribucion en los graficos. Para esto, necesitamos crear dos graficos separados que luego combinamos: Tambien podemos transformar los datos en categorias y graficar el grafico principal y el recuadro de esa manera.
code: # obtener datos de caracteristicas wad_subset <- get_wad_data() # recortar y normalizar datos wadOut <- make_normalised_raster(   raster_in = wad_subset,   pus = pus,   iso3 = "NPL" )  terra::plot(wadOut) (plot_wad <- elsar_plot_static_raster(   raster_in = wadOut,   legend_title = "WAD" )) custom_p <- ggplot2::scale_fill_distiller(   name = "WAD",   palette = "YlGnBu",   aesthetics = c("fill"),   oob = scales::squish )  (plot_wad <- elsar_plot_static_raster(   raster_in = wadOut,   custom_palette = custom_p,   background = wad_subset ) +   elsar_plot_optics()) #esquinaSuperiorDerecha x_min <- terra::ext(wadOut)[1][[1]] x_max <- terra::ext(wadOut)[2][[1]] y_min <- terra::ext(wadOut)[3][[1]] y_max <- terra::ext(wadOut)[4][[1]]  img_xmax <- x_max + x_max * 0.09 img_xmin <- img_xmax * 0.8 img_ymax <- y_max + y_max * 0.102 img_ymin <- img_ymax * 0.8  img_pos <- c(img_xmin, img_xmax, img_ymin, img_ymax)  (plot_out <- elsar_plot_static_raster(   raster_in = wadOut,   background = wad_subset,   legend_title = "WAD",   expand_plot = TRUE ) +   elsar_plot_optics(include_logo = TRUE,                       logo_path = "figures/elsaR_hex_sticker.png",                       logo_pos = img_pos,                       logo_dim = c(50, 60)))

title: Un Ejemplo de Flujo de Trabajo ELSA
text: Las areas protegidas pueden superponerse en mapas de caracteristicas para visualizar su relacion espacial con los valores de biodiversidad.
code: current_pas <- current_pas_sf  (plot_wad_pas <- elsar_plot_static_raster(   raster_in = wadOut,   background = wad_subset,   legend_title = "WAD" ) +   elsar_plot_extra_data(include_pas = current_pas, color_pa = "red") +   elsar_plot_optics()) (plot_wad_pas2 <- elsar_plot_static_raster(   raster_in = wadOut,   background = wad_subset,   legend_title = "WAD" ) +   elsar_plot_extra_data(include_pas = current_pas, pas_look = "area", color_pa = "grey") +   elsar_plot_optics(include_north_scale = TRUE))

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Este documento describe las convenciones de nomenclatura estandarizadas para los conjuntos de datos de entrada y salida utilizados en los analisis ELSA. Una nomenclatura consistente mejora la organizacion de archivos, facilita la identificacion de conjuntos de datos y asegura la reproducibilidad entre diferentes paises y niveles de analisis.

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Todo en minusculas – los nombres de archivo deben usar solo letras minusculas. Guiones bajos para espacios – use guiones bajos (_) para separar palabras. Sin caracteres especiales – evite guiones, puntos (excepto antes de la extension del archivo) u otros caracteres especiales. Sea descriptivo – el nombre del archivo debe transmitir el contenido, el alcance geografico y el nivel de analisis de un vistazo.

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Los analisis ELSA se organizan en tres niveles segun la fuente y resolucion de los datos de entrada: El codigo de nivel se agrega los nombres de archivo producidos por el pipeline de elsar para indicar que nivel de analisis genero la salida.

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Las salidas de las funciones de elsar siguen este patron: Ejemplos:
code: <descripcion>_<iso3>_<nivel>.<ext> protect_zone_hti_t2.tif restore_zone_zaf_t1.tif planning_units_kaz_t3.tif protected_areas_dom_t2.tif

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Los conjuntos de datos que varian en el tiempo (p. ej., uso/cobertura del suelo) incluyen el ano: Ejemplos:
code: <descripcion>_<ano>_<iso3>_<nivel>.<ext> esri_10m_proportion_2023_gha_t2.tif deforestation_risk_2020_dom_t2.tif

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Las capas de proporcion LULC adicionalmente incluyen la clase de cobertura del suelo: Ejemplos:
code: <producto>_proportion_<ano>_<iso3>_<clase>.<ext> esri_10m_proportion_2023_gha_agriculture.tif dynamic_world_proportion_2023_ken_built_area.tif

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Las salidas de graficos siguen la misma convencion con extension .png: Ejemplos:
code: <descripcion>_<iso3>.png protect_zone_hti.png forest_integrity_zaf.png

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Un diseno recomendado para organizar las salidas del analisis ELSA: inputs/ – conjuntos de datos de entrada crudos y preprocesados. outputs/ – todas las salidas generadas por el pipeline de elsar. reports/ – informes finales, resumenes y documentacion.
code: elsa_<iso3>/ ├── inputs/ │   ├── rasters/ │   └── vectors/ ├── outputs/ │   ├── rasters/ │   ├── vectors/ │   └── figures/ └── reports/

title: Convenciones de Nomenclatura de Archivos
text: Versionado – si existen multiples versiones de un conjunto de datos, agregue un numero de version la descripcion, o un termino especifico para el proyecto, p. ej., restoring_hope: protect_zone_v2_dom_t2.tif, protect_zone_restoring_hope_dom_t1.tif Marcas de fecha – si es necesario, agregue una fecha en formato AAAAMMDD: protect_zone_20240601_dom_t2.tif.

title: Functions and Tools For Creating ELSA Data Stacks
text: Esta traducción fue generada mediante IA. Consulte la versión en inglés como referencia principal. El paquete elsar proporciona herramientas y flujos de datos para apoyar la planificación de conservación de Áreas Esenciales de Soporte Vital (ELSA). Está diseñado para asistir en la priorización espacial y el desarrollo de escenarios utilizando capas de datos geoespaciales estandarizadas nivel global.

title: Functions and Tools For Creating ELSA Data Stacks
text: Preparar regiones de planificación: Crear límites y unidades de planificación para cualquier país Procesar conjuntos de datos globales: Cargar y armonizar conjuntos de datos espaciales (WDPCA, KBA, LANDMark, ICCA, etc.) Crear capas de zonas: Generar rásters de zonas de protección, restauración y gestión Calcular métricas: Calcular la cobertura espacial sobre unidades de planificación Visualizar resultados: Graficar datos ráster con estilos personalizables

title: Download and Process a GEE Raster Layer into a Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF — download_gee_layer
text: boundary_layer sf object defining spatial boundary interest. iso3 Character. Three-letter ISO country code filename generation. gee_project Character. Google Earth Engine cloud project ID. required must valid GEE project access . Find project ID GEE Code Editor. asset_id Character. Earth Engine ImageCollection asset ID (e.g., "projects/sat-io/open-datasets/..."). file_prefix Character. Prefix export filename GEE task description. output_dir Character. Path directory saving final raster file. Defaults project root via ::(). scale Numeric. Resolution exported image meters. Default 10. Ignored aggregate_to_pus = TRUE (uses PU resolution instead). datatype Character. Output datatype (GDAL style), e.g., "INT1U" "FLT4S". Default "INT1U". googledrive_folder Character NULL. Google Drive folder name exports. Currently defaults NULL (Drive root) avoid GEE folder duplication bug. wait_time Numeric. Maximum time minutes wait GEE export appear Google Drive. Default 5. Increase large exports. band Character NULL. Specific band select image. NULL, uses bands. Useful datasets like Dynamic World ("label") ESA WorldCover ("Map"). composite_method Character. Method creating temporal composite: "mosaic" (default), "mode" (frequent value), "median", "mean". Use "mode" categorical data like Dynamic World. year_override Numeric NULL. provided, use year instead automatically detecting recent year data. pus SpatRaster NULL. Planning units raster GEE-side aggregation. provided aggregate_to_pus = TRUE, export resampled match PU resolution projection GEE download. aggregate_to_pus Logical. TRUE pus provided, aggregate data planning unit resolution GEE export. significantly speeds processing eliminating local resampling. Default FALSE. aggregation_reducer Character. Reducer use GEE-side aggregation: "mode" (default, categorical data), "mean", "sum".

what: Calibrate feature weights for an ELSA prioritization — elsar_calibrate_weights
title: Calibrate feature weights for an ELSA prioritization — elsar_calibrate_weights
text: Iteratively computes set per-feature weights features achieve even representation relative attain (single-feature maximum). -represented features receive larger weights; well-represented features receive none. pre-calibration step ELSA workflow: resulting weights multiplied user-supplied weights deployed tool solves prioritization.

title: Calibrate feature weights for an ELSA prioritization — elsar_calibrate_weights
code: elsar_calibrate_weights(   features,   impacts,   zones_pu,   planning_units,   budgets,   lockins = NULL,   feature_totals = NULL,   wgt_scale = 2,   gap = 0.05,   solver = c("gurobi", "highs", "default"),   threads = 4,   time_limit = 3600,   solve_retries = 2,   progress_file = NULL,   freeze_tol = 0.01,   freeze_patience = 3,   max_iter = 200,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calibrate feature weights for an ELSA prioritization — elsar_calibrate_weights
text: features SpatRaster feature layers, ideally normalised [0, 1]. Layer names used feature identifiers throughout. impacts data.frame mapping features action zones. Must contain feature column (matching names(features)) one numeric column per action zone giving feature's zone-contribution ("impact"), typically [0, 1.5]. Zone column names must match names(zones_pu). zones_pu named list single-layer SpatRaster planning-unit masks (1 = available, NA = unavailable), one per action zone. lock-ins enforced, pass masks already exclude lock-cells zones. Names define action zones order. planning_units single-layer SpatRaster planning units study region (1/NA). Used objective budget denominator area targets. budgets named numeric vector per-zone area caps, percentage study-area planning-unit count. Names must match names(zones_pu). lockins Optional named list SpatRaster lock-areas per action zone. Used raise zone's target locked-coverage lock-exceeds nominal budget (feasibility floor). feature_totals Optional named numeric vector raw (non-impact-weighted) feature totals used representation denominator. Computed features via terra::global(..., "sum") NULL (default). wgt_scale Maximum weight increment applied per iteration (default 2). gap Solver optimality-gap tolerance (default 0.05). Note: ELSA max-utility problem typically solved exact optimum root node, tolerance rarely binds. solver Solver backend: "gurobi" (default), "highs", "default" (prioritizr's best available). "gurobi" requires gurobi package. threads Number solver threads (default 4). time_limit Per-solve time limit seconds (default 3600). solve_retries Number times retry failed solve (default 2), survive transient solver cloud-connection errors (e.g. dropped connection Gurobi Compute Server). short pause separates attempts. progress_file Optional path CSV. set, iteration's trajectory row appended completes, long-running calibration can monitored live (e.g. UI polling file). function otherwise side-effect-free. existing file path overwritten. freeze_tol Convergence threshold maximum per-feature change representation iterations (default 0.01). allocation stops changing , additive reweighting move (exactly solved) optimum. freeze_patience Number consecutive frozen iterations required stop (default 3). max_iter Safety cap number calibration iterations (default 200). verbose Logical; emit progress messages via log_message() (default TRUE).

title: Calibrate feature weights for an ELSA prioritization — elsar_calibrate_weights
text: named list class elsar_calibration elements: weights tibble columns feature weight (calibrated per-feature weight). weight_matrix feature x zone weight matrix actually used (do_nothing column 0). representation tibble per-feature (achieved representation), a_max, ratio = / a_max, delta_pct shortfall. a_max Named numeric vector single-feature maximum representations. trajectory tibble per-iteration diagnostics (spread, max change, weight range, shortfall summary). converged Logical; TRUE loop stopped freeze rule rather max_iter. iterations Number calibration iterations run. meta list run metadata (solver, gap, wgt_scale, package versions).

title: Calibrate feature weights for an ELSA prioritization — elsar_calibrate_weights
text: routine reproduces ELSA calibration framework exactly (new objective): (1) feature's maximum attainable representation a_max obtained solving zoned maximum-utility problem feature alone weighted; (2) problem solved equal weights obtain feature's baseline representation relative shortfall delta = (- a_max) / a_max * 100; (3) weights adjusted iteratively additive, increase-rule adj = wgt_scale - (delta - min(delta)) / (max(delta) - min(delta)) * wgt_scale, re-solving recomputing shortfalls, allocation stops changing. weight set giving even representation (smallest spread delta) returned. Requires prioritizr package (supported solver, e.g. gurobi highs); optional dependencies.

title: Diagnose your elsar setup — elsar_check_setup
text: elsar three tiers different setup requirements (see package's SUPPORT.md): Core - boundaries, planning units, normalisation, zone builders local rasters, plotting. Needs geospatial R stack (GDAL, GEOS, PROJ, installed sf/terra). Works offline. Protected areas - make_protected_areas(from_wdpca = TRUE), needs wdpar, Chromium browser, libarchive system library. Earth Engine - download_* functions, need reticulate, googledrive, conda/Python environment earthengine-api, Google Earth Engine account approved Cloud project. filing bug report download_* functions, paste output elsar_check_setup() report.

title: Extract and Filter IUCN GET Ecosystem Vector Layers — get_iucn_ecosystems
text: iucn_get_directory Character. Path directory containing IUCN GET .gpkg files. iso3 Character. ISO3 country code (used logging naming output file). pus SpatRaster object. Used defining spatial extent target CRS. boundary_layer sf polygon layer (typically country boundary) used spatially crop features. include_minor_occurrence Logical. FALSE, filters ecosystems marked minor occurrence (default TRUE). iucn_get_prefixes Optional character vector GET ecosystem IDs include (e.g., c("F1.1", "TF1.2")). NULL, layers included. excluded_prefixes Optional character vector ecosystem prefixes exclude (e.g., c("T7.1", "T7.2") intensive land-use biomes). Default exclude T7 classes. output_path Character NULL. specified, writes resulting sf object GeoPackage directory.

title: Extract and Filter IUCN GET Ecosystem Vector Layers — get_iucn_ecosystems
text: sf object merged, reprojected, valid IUCN GET ecosystem features intersecting target boundary.

title: Extract and Filter IUCN GET Ecosystem Vector Layers — get_iucn_ecosystems
text: function reads, intersects, reprojects, merges .gpkg vector files representing IUCN Global Ecosystem Typology (GET) layers specified directory. allows filtering filename prefixes (e.g., "T1.2", "TF1.4"), excludes specific ecosystem types (e.g., intensive land-use biomes), optionally removes features minor occurrence flags. resulting layer cropped country boundary reprojected match planning units raster. ecosystem layer ID (e.g., "F1.1") extracted source filename added feature get_id column tracking. output_path specified, final filtered valid sf object saved GeoPackage.

title: Extract and Filter IUCN GET Ecosystem Vector Layers — get_iucn_ecosystems
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Define inputs pus <- terra::rast("data/pus_raster.tif") boundary <- sf::st_read("data/country_boundary.gpkg")  # Get all layers except intensive land-use (T7) and minor occurrences ecosystems <- get_iucn_ecosystems(   iucn_get_directory = "data/iucn_layers",   iso3 = "KEN",   boundary_layer = boundary,   pus = pus,   include_minor_occurrence = FALSE,   excluded_prefixes = c("T7.1", "T7.2", "T7.3", "T7.4", "T7.5"),   output_path = "outputs" ) } # }

title: Create a boundary of the planning region — make_boundary
text: boundary_in file containing boundary information. Can sf SpatRaster input_type string either "sf" "SpatRaster" (default "sf"). limit_to_mainland Logical. TRUE, keep single largest polygon (mainland), dropping islands outlying parts. Applied iso3 filter, largest polygon chosen within target region. Default FALSE. col_name string column containing actual extent planning region (outside area). Can NULL. filter_out value representing outside area data (e.g. 0) custom_projection Logical. TRUEif custom projection planning region wanted. iso3 iso3 country code (character) country interest. iso3_column relevant iso3 != NULL. string name iso3 information can found dataset. filter_by_iso3 Logical. TRUE (default) iso3 supplied, boundary filtered feature(s) iso3_column == iso3. Set FALSE use whole input (e.g. custom regional boundary already clipped study area) still passing iso3 projection name. dissolve Logical. TRUE, dissolve (optionally filtered) features single geometry whole input treated one planning region. Useful multi-feature regional boundaries (e.g. several provinces). Default FALSE. output_path optional output path created file. needed custom_projection = TRUE.

what: Create a Planning Units Raster — make_planning_units
title: Create a Planning Units Raster — make_planning_units
text: Generates planning units (PUs) raster spatial prioritization using user-defined boundary. pu_size provided, function automatically estimates appropriate resolution (meters) keeps number planning units specified threshold (pu_threshold), allowing configurable tolerance (default 5%). finest resolution meeting constraint selected using adaptive increments clean rounding steps. result can optionally saved Cloud Optimized GeoTIFF (.tif).

title: Create a Planning Units Raster — make_planning_units
code: make_planning_units(   boundary_proj,   pu_size = NULL,   pu_threshold = 850000,   pu_tolerance = 0.05,   limit_to_mainland = FALSE,   iso3,   background_fill = NA,   output_path = NULL )

title: Create a Planning Units Raster — make_planning_units
text: boundary_proj projected sf object representing boundary planning region. Must projected CRS linear units (typically meters). Use make_boundary() generate . pu_size Optional numeric. Length meters square planning unit. NULL, function estimates size keeps total number PUs pu_threshold. pu_threshold Numeric. Maximum number planning units allowed (default 850,000). soft threshold pu_threshold * (1 + pu_tolerance) used optimization. pu_tolerance Numeric. Fractional tolerance (default 0.05, 5%) pu_threshold allowed estimating PU size automatically. limit_to_mainland Logical. Reserved future use. TRUE, limits planning units mainland regions . iso3 Character. ISO3 country code used name output raster (e.g., "KEN", "BRA"). background_fill value apply pixels outside boundary_proj. default NA (e.g., nodata). good reason wanting use different value. output_path Optional character. Directory path save resulting raster. NULL, raster saved.

title: Create a Planning Units Raster — make_planning_units
text: single-layer SpatRaster object terra package. non-zero cells represent valid planning units.

text: pus SpatRaster. Planning units raster defining output resolution extent. iso3 Character. ISO3 country code (e.g., "CHL"). from_wdpca Logical. Required from_wdpca = FALSE. download_path Character NULL. Directory WDPCA data saved loaded . Required from_wdpca = TRUE. status Character vector. STATUS values include WDPCA data. TRUE, creates geodesic buffers around point geometries using area attribute. Default TRUE. area_column Character. Column name containing area values buffering point geometries. title: elsar (development version)
text: elsar_calibrate_weights() computes per-feature calibration weights ELSA prioritization features achieve even representation relative single-feature maxima. ELSA pre-calibration step, previously living downstream tool; objective framework unchanged (spread--shortfall minimisation via additive, increase-weight update), added robustness: returns best-seen weights, stops allocation freezes, excludes zero-total features instead aborting. returns structured elsar_calibration object (weights, representation profile, per-iteration trajectory, metadata) writes files. prioritizr solver (gurobi, highs free/local option) optional dependencies, checked call time actionable message.

title: elsar (development version)
text: make_protected_areas(from_wdpca = TRUE) requires wdpar; download_* (Earth Engine) functions require reticulate googledrive (run elsar_setup_gee() configure ). scales png moved Suggests Imports: used unconditionally core plotting functions, belong .

title: elsar (development version)
text: elsar now loads sf namespace loaded, sf’s S3 methods (e.g. [.sf, filter.sf) registered. Previously package referred sf via sf:: never imported , sf’s namespace loaded package-load time. result, operations sf objects - including subsetting bundled boundary_dat dplyr calls inside make_boundary() - dispatch wrong method fail vctrs “incompatible scalar type” error unless sf happened loaded way (e.g. library(sf)). Surfaced running tests installed package R CMD check. make_planning_units() now errors clearly boundary_proj geographic (lat/long) CRS CRS. Previously lat/long boundary silently rasterised single cell (metre resolution interpreted degrees) every downstream step ran one cell error warning. Use make_boundary(custom_projection = TRUE) (default) get suitable projected boundary. make_boundary(limit_to_mainland = TRUE) now works. previously always errored (sf::st_area() called rlang data pronoun). now keeps single largest polygon, iso3 filter, mainland chosen within target region rather across whole input dataset. result also normalised WGS84 non-mainland path already . make_boundary() now errors actionable message (listing available codes) iso3 matches feature, instead silently returning 0-row sf (later failing cryptic GDAL projection error). also checks iso3_column / col_name actually columns input, boundary coordinate reference system reprojecting . elsar_load_data() longer silently returns non-sf object reads fail. loading multiple files/layers now errors every read fails (previously returned 0 x 0 tibble logging “Loaded 0 features”), warns continues fail, naming . Loading single unreadable file now errors instead returning NULL. Relatedly, filter_sf() now honours “return NULL failure” contract corrupt files: layer-name probe (sf::st_layers()) now inside read guard, bad file skipped rather aborting whole load. successfully-read layers different coordinate reference systems, load now stops clear message naming instead failing deep inside bind_rows(). elsar_load_data()’s PostgreSQL path now works. connection list spliced DBI::dbConnect() via .call() instead rlang !!! operator, never worked (dbConnect() plain S4 generic, !!!x parsed !(!(!x))). path now also guards RPostgres/DBI install hint, closes connection .exit(), quotes table/column identifiers iso3 literal prevent SQL injection, omits clause iso3/iso3_column supplied.

title: elsar (development version)
text: New elsar_check_setup() diagnoses installation tier tier (core / protected areas / Earth Engine) reports present, missing, exact command fix gap. makes changes, needs credentials, never errors, safe run time - output bug-report template asks Earth Engine users include. New elsar_setup_gee() bootstraps Earth Engine environment one call: finds conda (can install miniconda allow ), creates persistent conda environment (elsar_ee) earthengine-api, optionally walks Earth Engine Google Drive authentication. Run , ahead time, instead letting first download_* call build environment mid-analysis.

title: elsar (development version)
text: Earth Engine conda environment now persistent reused across sessions rather created per-process removed download. Previously download_* call build gee_temp_env_<pid> environment delete exit, re-downloading Python earthengine-api every session (, run interrupted cleanup, leaving stray gee_temp_env_* environments behind). initialize_earthengine() now creates reuses stable elsar_ee environment. Conda detection (find_conda_base()) now also consults reticulate (managed miniconda, conda_binary(), miniconda_path()), installations non-standard locations - including reticulate-installed miniconda micromamba/Homebrew installs - found instead triggering “find conda installation”.

title: elsar 0.4.0
text: Zone-function parameters harmonised coherent API. human-pressure input now human_pressure everywhere (hii_input make_protect_zone(), make_manage_zone(), make_degraded_areas()); raw input rasters use bare names without _input suffix (agricultural_areas, built_areas, pasturelands, managed_forests - previously *_input); make_degraded_areas() now takes iso3/pus (country_iso/pu); built-area threshold built_areas_threshold everywhere (built_area_threshold make_restore_zone() / make_urban_greening_zone()); make_restore_zone()’s patch filter filter_patch_size (filter_small_patches). Callers must update named arguments accordingly. make_degraded_areas() writes final output degraded_areas_{iso3}.tif (restore_zone_{iso3}.tif, easily confused make_restore_zone()’s restore_zones_{iso3}.tif).

title: elsar 0.4.0
text: protect: agriculture_areas_, built_areas_, human_pressure_ restore: agriculture_areas_, built_areas_, human_pressure_, degradation_, forest_mask_ manage: agriculture_areas_, pasturelands_, built_areas_, hii_mid60pct_, managed_forests_ urban greening: built_areas_ intermediate terra::mask()-ed planning units, cells outside study area NoData (consistent zone outputs).

title: elsar 0.4.0
text: make_degraded_areas() now writes resampled agriculture built-area sidecars (esa_agriculture_resample_, built_areas_) FLT4S. bilinear proportions (0-1) previously saved INT1U, truncated -zero. make_restore_zone() now always saves agriculture_areas_ built_areas_ intermediates output_path set, including derived LULC (previously saved passed explicit rasters). make_normalised_raster() longer emits warning() every rescaled = FALSE call (demoted informational message), internal output_path writer uses valid INT1S NoData sentinel (-128) instead --range 255.

title: elsar 0.3.1
text: exact_rasterise() longer exhausts OS open-file limit crawls vector layers many features. Previously built one coverage-fraction raster per feature stacked , O(nfeatures) open file handles time - wall--wall class maps tens thousands polygons failed “open N files”. new max_exact_features argument (default 1000) switches large layers standard fun (mean/sum/max/min) single terra::rasterize() pass; exact per-feature coverage path retained smaller layers. large layer non-standard fun now warns falling back.

title: elsar 0.3.0
text: make_custom_projection() renamed make_custom_mollweide_projection() (projection always Mollweide). old name still works deprecated alias warns.

title: elsar 0.3.0
text: Regional / sub-national planning runs: iso3 may carry suffix (e.g. "ECU_REG", "ECU-GEF8"). is_valid_iso3() functions validate now accept custom regional codes, rely supplied boundary rather ISO lookup. make_boundary() make_custom_mollweide_projection() support whole-file regional boundaries: use custom boundary ISO3 column, dissolving multiple features single planning region. save_raster() now builds overviews (OVERVIEWS=AUTO) outputs fully valid Cloud Optimized GeoTIFFs, gains overview-resampling override (resampling=, e.g. "mode" categorical layers). make_protected_areas() tolerates custom local protected-area layers lack WDPCA schema (STATUS / SITE_TYPE / MAB filters apply columns exist).

title: elsar 0.3.0
text: make_custom_mollweide_projection() computes projection centre WGS84, fixing wrong central meridian input boundary projected CRS (e.g. UTM); also tolerates NULL iso3. CRS-aware spatial filter: elsar_load_data() / filter_sf() reproject wkt_filter geometry layer’s CRS filtering, filter built one CRS longer returns zero features layer stored another (e.g. Mollweide gpkg vs planning units different central meridian). make_normalised_raster() masks unflagged NoData sentinels (e.g. -2147483648, -2147483647) otherwise dominate 0-1 rescale flatten real values ~1; warns .

title: elsar 0.2.0
text: Renamed from_wdpa parameter from_wdpca make_protected_areas() reflect rename World Database Protected Areas (WDPA) World Database Protected Conserved Areas (WDPCA).

title: elsar 0.2.0
text: Added file naming conventions vignette documenting standardised naming patterns ELSA pipeline inputs, outputs, figures. Added dark mode toggle pkgdown website. Added make_urban_greening_zone() pkgdown reference index. make_protect_zone() now adapts HII threshold mean HII inside protected areas high (>= 4). Uses PA mean threshold instead quantile-based approach avoid excluding nearly areas high-HFI countries (#79). hii_input now optional.

title: elsar 0.2.0

Documented removed/deprecated datasets dedicated section. Added version tracking column dataset reference table.
